title: ForgeBranchVerdict
description: Structured output returned by one stress-test agent for one load-bearing branch of a forged idea. Use as the StructuredOutput schema when fanning out via the Agent tool or the Workflow tool.

branch.description: The load-bearing claim this agent was assigned to attack, restated in one sharp sentence.

verdict.description: VIABLE = holds against the strongest attack. ADAPT = the broad claim fails but a reshaped version holds; the finding says how to change. PRUNE = falsified, irrelevant, or owned by someone the idea-holder can't out-execute — a dead end.

failure_mode.description: How the branch failed (drives the adaptation). 'none' iff verdict is VIABLE. 'false' = factually wrong. 'irrelevant' = true but doesn't change the outcome. 'already-owned' = true but an incumbent/OSS/standard/vendor owns it.

claim.description: The single most damaging concrete finding — a named product, paper, funding round, regulation with date, or benchmark number.

source.description: Citation: URL, paper, or named source. Required for every non-obvious claim.

confidence.description: Established = multi-source. Reported = single/secondary. Vendor/Projection = marketing/forecast. Contested = sources disagree. Open = unresolved.

adaptation.description: How the idea should change given the finding. Grounded and incremental — reshape, do not lurch. Empty allowed only when verdict is VIABLE.

surviving_edge.description: The strongest version of the claim the damaging finding does not reach — what still stands.

opportunity_signals.description: Incumbent holes and unmet demand mined from reviews / complaints / forums / churn — wedge openings. May be empty.

signal.description: The gap or unmet want, stated sharply.

source.description: Where it was observed (review site, forum thread, churn post, etc.).
